title: Plugin
description: A Plugin entity represents a plugin configuration that will be executed during the HTTP request/response lifecycle. Plugins provide extra functionality such as authentication, rate limiting, and transformations.

id.description: The unique identifier of the Plugin.

name.description: The name of the plugin (e.g., rate-limiting, key-auth, cors).

instance_name.description: An optional custom name to identify an instance of the plugin.

config.description: The configuration properties for the Plugin which vary depending on the plugin being configured.

protocols.description: A list of the request protocols that will trigger this plugin.

tags.description: An optional set of strings for grouping and filtering.

ordering.description: Describes a dependency to another plugin to determine plugin ordering during the access phase (Enterprise only).

service.description: If set, the plugin will only activate when receiving requests via the specified Service.

route.description: If set, the plugin will only activate when receiving requests via the specified Route.

consumer.description: If set, the plugin will activate only for requests where the specified Consumer has been authenticated.

consumer_group.description: If set, the plugin will activate only for requests within the specified Consumer Group.

created_at.description: Unix epoch timestamp of when the entity was created.

updated_at.description: Unix epoch timestamp of when the entity was last updated.
